content.text:
Portfolio Review & Market Scan (3/9/2026, 3:04 PM):

- Holdings: NVDA (2), TSLA (1), AAPL (2), AMD (2), PLTR (1). All are prominent tech/growth leaders.
- Prices: All positions are currently slightly down from their recent purchase prices, but not by a drastic margin.
- Cash available: €95.42—not enough for substantial new buys in most well-known stocks.

Analysis:
- Attempts to scan for urgent market news or top movers encountered technical problems today.
- Without real-time momentum or market catalyst data, and seeing no major warning signs among high-quality holdings, the prudent move is to hold. None are drastically underperforming, and these names historically rebound well.
- No panic selling or forced averaging down. Maintaining a small cash reserve preserves flexibility for the next session when real-time scans are possible.

Action:
- No trades at this time; holding current positions.
- Will resume search for opportunities and news on the next run when market data access is restored.

Summary:
- Stay the course with strong names through short-term volatility.
- Next trading decision will be made after successful market scan, with ongoing risk management and performance tracking.

Ready to act more aggressively when market tools are restored or if fresh data emerges!
